format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is the H1 title. All six fixed sections present in correct order. reading.meta.json parses cleanly with all required fields.

length_budget.notes: 983 words (target 800–1200). ceil(983/200) = 5 min (target 4–6 min). Both within range.

citations.notes: [1] cited 11 times (satisfies ≥1 primary-resource requirement). [2] and [3] each have matching bibliography entries. No orphan markers or entries.

no_forward_references.notes: All concepts in the reading are either prior-topic concepts (system prompt, role assignment, few-shot examples, constraints, output format control, anthropic SDK, client.messages.create) or this topic's own concepts (5-role context framework, context package, authority role, exemplar role, constraint role, rubric role, metadata role, priority stack, conflict resolution). f-string is introduced inline with a plain-English definition at line 179 and is basic Python syntax from the M5 prior weeks — not a forward reference.

faithfulness_to_corpus.notes: All 10 sampled assertions (priority stack definition, new-employee analogy, missing-role consequences table, recipe assistant worked example, Python dict structure, f-string usage for Metadata, conflict resolution rule, pitfall list, authority stability rule, exemplar-vs-constraint conflict rule) trace directly to corpus sections 1, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8.

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms (context package, priority stack, f-string, context package) are defined with bold-term + plain-meaning shape on first use. Each role is introduced via a concrete analogy (new employee documents) before the abstract definition. Sentences are concise; active voice; learner addressed as 'you'. No hedging phrases detected. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously.

formatting.notes: Key Concepts section uses sub-sections with bullet lists and inline code snippets per role. In Practice section uses numbered steps 1–5 with code blocks. Priority table rendered as a Markdown table. No paragraph exceeds 4–5 sentences. Inline examples appear in Key Concepts alongside concept introduction, not only in Worked Example. Bold-term definitions used consistently throughout.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in the reading.

scope.notes: Reading teaches exactly the 5-role context framework: five named roles, priority stack, conflict resolution, and Python assembly. No adjacent or advanced concepts (RAG, agent frameworks, fine-tuning, tool use) are taught or defined beyond a bare name. The f-string explanation is within scope as it is the assembly mechanism for Metadata in Python.

escalation_check.notes: Zero soft findings across checks 5–7. No escalation triggered.

gold_example_comparison.notes: No gold example exists for topic 13.7 or the M5 prompt-engineering module. Gold examples present in .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/ are for topics 2-1 and 3-4. Written standards in artifact-mix.json used as the pass bar instead.

summary: All eight checks pass. The reading is within word-count and time budget (983 words / 5 min, atomic profile). Primary resource [1] is cited 11 times. No forward references. All sampled assertions are faithful to the corpus. The reading is beginner-accessible: concrete-first, terms defined on first use, no jargon dumps, no walls of prose, no assessment metadata, and scope is tightly held to the 5-role context framework headline.
